Package neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.flashops

Class TVflash

java.lang.Object
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.BaseOperation
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.flashops.Flash
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.flashops.TVflash
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Runnable, OperationInterface
public class TVflash extends Flash
Temperature-Volume flash calculation.

Given temperature T and volume V, solves for pressure P using Newton iteration. The calculation iteratively adjusts pressure until the system volume matches the specified volume.

Version:
$Id: $Id
Author:
even solbraa

  • Method Details

    • calcdQdVdP

      public double calcdQdVdP()
      Calculate dV/dP at constant T and n (derivative of volume with respect to pressure).

      For multiphase systems, the total derivative is the sum of individual phase derivatives.

      Returns:
      dV/dP in cm³/bar

    • calcdQdV

      public double calcdQdV()
      Calculate volume residual (current volume - specified volume).
      Returns:
      volume residual in cm³

    • estimatePressureFromTV

      private double estimatePressureFromTV()
      Compute an initial pressure estimate using the TV specification approach.

      Uses a clone of the system set to TV mode (constant volume, single-phase). The EOS then computes pressure analytically from the Helmholtz free energy derivative (P = -dF/dV + nRT/V) without iterating on volume. This is the same approach used by gradient theory for interfacial tension calculations.

      This only works reliably for single-phase systems. For two-phase states the EOS will compute a pressure corresponding to the metastable single-phase root, but this still provides a much better initial guess than the arbitrary starting pressure.

      Returns:
      estimated pressure in bar, or -1 if the estimate is invalid

    • solveQ

      public double solveQ()
      Solve for pressure that gives the specified volume using Newton iteration.

      The algorithm first attempts a fast single-phase pressure estimate using the TV specification approach (same as gradient theory): given T and V, the EOS computes P analytically via calcPressure() on a clone. If the system turns out to be single-phase, this converges in 1-2 iterations. For two-phase systems, it provides a good initial guess for the Newton iteration.

      The Newton loop uses 1/10 damped analytical dV/dP derivatives with 2x max step safety bounds. Early termination when error starts growing (error &lt; errorOld) prevents oscillation near phase boundaries.

      Returns:
      converged pressure in bar

    • getIterations

      public int getIterations()
      Get the number of iterations from the last calculation.
      Returns:
      number of iterations

      public void run()
